name: Intuneomator
description: Intuneomator is a macOS enterprise tool that automates Microsoft Intune app lifecycle management, leveraging Installomator’s 900+ label database for multi-arch apps. It enables end-to-end workflows (download, package, upload, deploy), group targeting, metadata and script automation, plus Teams-based status and CVE alerts. Built with a secure XPC architecture, keychain-backed credentials, and dual authentication for enterprise-grade security.
keywords: how to automate mac app uploads
author: Gil Burns

securityCheck.aiSummary: The Intuneomator codebase largely implements standard macOS packaging and release workflows (label processing, package building, signing, and notarization). Key concerns identified: (1) label processing uses eval to load label data which can enable code execution if labels aren’t strictly controlled; (2) the integrate-daemon-sync.sh script injects a build-phase shell script into the Xcode project, creating a potential attack surface if project files are tampered with; (3) signing identifiers are hard-coded in build scripts, which could be exposed if the repository is compromised. No explicit credential harvesting, data exfiltration, or remote code execution patterns were detected in these excerpts. Recommend hardening input parsing, build-script integrity, and externalizing secrets to secure storage.
